name: Earl King
abstract: Earl Silas Johnson IV (February 7, 1934 – April 17, 2003), known as Earl King, was an American singer, guitarist, and songwriter, most active in blues music. A composer of blues standards such as "Come On" (covered by Jimi Hendrix, Freddie King, Stevie Ray Vaughan) and "Big Chief" (recorded by Professor Longhair), he was an important fig…

about_quotes.evidence: Actually, I had been playing with Earl King and Roland Cook, who was the bass player for Earl King.

about_quotes.evidence: Earl King to go as Guitar Slim... We had played in Flint, Michigan. We had played up there with the regular band with Slim before this happened... we went to Bessma, Alabama with Earl as Slim

about_quotes.speaker: Professor Longhair (Henry Roeland Byrd)
about_quotes.evidence: Earl King, I just helped him out with it. ... I had to rearrange some of the things. To fit what I was putting into his song you know.

about_quotes.speaker: George "Tex" Stephens
about_quotes.evidence: People would, Earl King would probably leave the Dew Drop and go over to the Orange, Texas, and play either Smile or Lewis.
